{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Silencer-7'', this is Imperial Star Destroyer ''Allegiance''! Rebel forces have—|Last transmission from the ''Allegiance''|audio=Allegiance.flac|<ref name="DEaudio" />}}
The '''''Allegiance''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Allegiance-class battlecruiser|''Allegiance''-class battlecruiser]] that participated in the [[Battle of Calamari]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. When the ''Allegiance'' began transmitting images of Calamari's destruction to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' intercepted the transmissions and tracked the ''Allegiance''{{'s}} location. Once the ''Emancipator'' dropped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] above Calamari, it attacked and destroyed the ''Allegiance''.

==Description==
The ''Allegiance'' was a [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] that served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] after the [[Cloning/Legends|rebirth]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="DE3" /> The [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] served as the Imperial [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] during the [[Battle of Calamari]],<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> where it transmitted images of [[Dac/Legends|Calamari's]] destruction to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] through a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] communications channel. The ''Allegiance'' was also equipped with [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]].<ref name="DE3" />

==History==
{{Quote|We've done it! That Super Star Destroyer was caught with its deflector shields down, it's hit!|Wedge Antilles|audio=Allegiance2.flac|<ref name="DEaudio" />}}
[[File:Emancipator-DE1.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The ''Emancipator'' passes in front of the drifting wreckage of the ''Allegiance''.]]
In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the ''Allegiance'' commanded a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]<ref name="SWE" /> [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Planet/Legends|world]] Calamari. It transmitted images of Calamari's subjugation by the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] latest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]], the [[World Devastator]]s, through a hyperspace communications channel to the Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] in the Deep Core. These transmissions were intercepted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' under the command of [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. The ''Emancipator'', already on its way to Calamari with a fleet of its own, recalculated its exit from hyperspace two degrees from the source of the transmissions.<ref name="DE3" />

As the ''Emancipator'' and its fleet dropped out of hyperspace, a [[Unidentified Allegiance crew member|crew member]] aboard the ''Allegiance'' attempted to contact the World Devastator ''[[Silencer-7]]'', but his transmission was cut off mid-sentence as the ''Emancipator'' opened fire on the Super Star Destroyer.<ref name="DEaudio" /> The ''Allegiance'', caught with its deflector shields down, was easily destroyed, alongside its two [[Procursator-class Star Destroyer|''Procursator''-class Star Destroyer]] escorts, by concentrated fire from the ''Emancipator''{{'s}} forward weapons, manned by [[Zevulon Veers]], and its wreckage was left to drift in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] above Calamari.<ref name="DE3" />

==Commanders and crew==
At least one crew member served aboard the ''Allegiance''. This crew member sent a transmission to ''Silencer-7'', warning [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Titus Klev]] of the New Republic attack on the Imperial fleet above Calamari. However, the crew member's message was cut short as the ''Emancipator'' fired on the ''Allegiance''.<ref name="DEaudio" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Finally, there is a Super Star Destroyer, the ''Allegiance'', though her commander remains unknown; the ''Allegiance''—along with another unnamed Super Star Destroyer of apparently unique design—was destroyed in the ''Dark Empire'' comic book series.|[[Eric S. Trautmann]], after being asked the names of every existing Super Star Destroyer in the ''[[ISB Intercepts]]'' question and answer column|<ref name="ISB Intercepts">{{JournalCite|15|ISB Intercepts}}</ref>}}
The ''Allegiance'' was created for the [[Dark Empire 3|third issue]] of the [[1991]]-[[1992]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]].<ref name="DE3" /> It later appeared in the [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|audio adaptation of ''Dark Empire'']], released in [[1994]].<ref name="DEaudio" /> The ''Allegiance'', compared with the other Star Destroyers surrounding it in issue three of ''Dark Empire'', is notably small for a Super Star Destroyer and resembles an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] in appearance.<ref name="DE3" /> At one point, the audio drama refers to the ''Allegiance'' as an "Imperial Star Destroyer," while it is called a Super Star Destroyer in all other instances.<ref name="DEaudio" />
{{Quote|Allegiance-class battlecruiser – 2.2 km long, often known as a Heavy Star Destroyer. This is the mysterious oversized ISD from Dark Empire II. It's also a perfect example of the "Star Cruiser" subclass of battlecruiser mentioned in Warfare.|[[Jason Fry]] in his EG to Warfare Endnotes Part 7|<ref name="JFT" />}}
The ''Allegiance''{{'s}} ship class was originally supplied by fan artist [[Ansel Hsiao]],<ref name="Fractalsponge original comment" /> who labeled it as the ''Allegiance''-class heavy Star Destroyer on his personal art gallery. The class was eventually [[List of fanon elements in continuity|made canon]] due to [[Lucas Licensing]] using Hsiao's artwork for the ships in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. The class name was also added into the Holocron's continuity database during this process, according to the artist.<ref name="Fractalsponge comments">{{WebCite|url=https://bbs.stardestroyer.net/viewtopic.php?f=46&t=124353&sid=e8501d9d9e2ccc9a1c18226a2628097f&start=1575|text=Something big - Page 64|work=stardestroyer.net|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/Xcz6}}</ref>

The use of "heavy" in the class-name on his gallery was Hsiao's own invention. According to him, it is not part of the canonical name which was submitted to the Lucas Licensing continuity database.<ref name="Fractalsponge comment2">{{WebCite|url=http://bbs.stardestroyer.net/viewtopic.php?f=46&t=124353&start=1623|text=Stardestroyer.net art thread. Hsiao explains that the term "heavy" is not part of its canon name.|archivedate=20200526055233}}</ref> The term "heavy star destroyer" was kept in canon as a nickname for the ship's class, according to author Jason Fry in his endnotes for the book. Although he refers to it as appearing in ''Dark Empire II'', the class name used in his endnotes is the same one Hsiao submitted. Fry used the "Anaxes class designation" for the ship, rather than the designation Hsiao has on his 3D model site. A similar change was made with the [[Bellator-class dreadnought/Legends|''Bellator''-class dreadnought]], which was originally listed as a Star Battlecruiser on Hsiao's site.<ref name="JFT" />
